lede: A look at December 26 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Josh Wilson-Esbrand and Jeroen Soete, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2015 Garland tornado
events.desc: A violent EF-4 tornado hits Garland, Texas, killing nine and injuring almost 500 others.

events.title: 2006 Hengchun earthquakes
events.desc: Two earthquakes in Hengchun, Taiwan measuring 7.0 and 6.9 on the moment magnitude scale kill two and disrupt telecommunications across Asia.

events.title: 2004 Indian Ocean earthquake and tsunami
events.desc: The 9.1–9.3 Mw Indian Ocean earthquake shakes northern Sumatra with a maximum Mercalli intensity of IX (Violent). One of the largest observed tsunamis, it affected coastal and partially mainland areas of Thailand, India, Sri Lanka, the Maldives, Malaysia, Myanmar, Bangladesh, and Indonesia; death toll is estimated at 227,898.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: German warship Scharnhorst is sunk off Norway's North Cape after a battle against major Royal Navy forces.

events.title: Babe Ruth
events.desc: Babe Ruth of the Boston Red Sox is sold to the New York Yankees by owner Harry Frazee, allegedly establishing the Curse of the Bambino superstition.

events.title: Thespis (opera)
events.desc: Thespis, the first Gilbert and Sullivan collaboration, debuts.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The Trent Affair: Confederate diplomatic envoys James Murray Mason and John Slidell are freed by the United States government, thus easing tensions between the U.S. and the United Kingdom.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: In the Battle of Trenton, the Continental Army under General George Washington executes a successful surprise attack and defeats a garrison of Hessian forces serving Great Britain.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: Bach led the first performance of Darzu ist erschienen der Sohn Gottes, BWV 40, his first Christmas Cantata composed for Leipzig.

events.title: Agrippina (opera)
events.desc: The opera Agrippina by George Frideric Handel premiered in Venice.

births.name: Evelyn Bark
births.line: leading member of the British Red Cross, first female recipient of the CMG (died 1993)

deaths.text: Leo Tindemans, Belgian politician, 43rd Prime Minister of Belgium
Glenn McCarthy, American businessman, founded the Shamrock Hotel
Antoine Houdar de la Motte, French author

observances.text: Boxing Day, except when December 26 is a Sunday. If it is a Sunday, Boxing Day is transferred to December 27 by Royal Proclamation. (Commonwealth of Nations), and its related observances: Day of Good Will (South Africa and Namibia)

Boxing Day, except when December 26 is a Sunday. If it is a Sunday, Boxing Day is transferred to December 27 by Royal Proclamation. (Commonwealth of Nations), and its related observances: Family Day (Vanuatu)

Boxing Day, except when December 26 is a Sunday. If it is a Sunday, Boxing Day is transferred to December 27 by Royal Proclamation. (Commonwealth of Nations), and its related observances: Thanksgiving (Solomon Islands)
Christian feast day: Abadiu of Antinoe (Coptic Church)
Christian feast day: Earliest day on which Feast of the Holy Family can fall, celebrated on Sunday after Christmas or 30 if Christmas falls on a Sunday.[citation needed]
Christian feast day: James the Just (Eastern Orthodox Church)
